title: Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate submission timeliness (covered by 22.39.1); focuses on factual consistency across parallel submissions.
evidence: Saved search 'regulator_submission_divergence' (post-submission) compares affected-subject and data-class counts across submissions sharing the same incident_id.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.33(3) requires specific material facts; divergence across submissions can be challenged.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.33(3) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.39.4: Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.39.4' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 requires a final report with same material facts; inconsistency is a deficiency.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.23(4) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.39.4: Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.39.4' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.19 initial and final reports must be internally consistent.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.19 (Reporting of major ICT-related incidents) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.39.4: Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.39.4' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.33(3) (parent assurance='partial'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that UK GDPR Art.33(3) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.39.4: Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.39.4' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Two submissions for incident_id=INC-123 report 1,000 and 10,000 affected subjects respectively; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Submissions with aligned subject counts do not fire.

dataSources: regulator:submission sourcetype records with normalised material-fact fields (affected_subjects, data_classes, root_cause).
spl:
index=notifications sourcetype=regulator:submission earliest=-30d
| stats values(affected_subjects) as subjects_set values(data_classes) as data_set dc(affected_subjects) as subjects_uniques dc(data_classes) as data_uniques by incident_id
| where subjects_uniques>1 OR data_uniques>1
| table incident_id subjects_set data_set
description: Reads the structured material-fact fields of every regulator submission for an incident and alerts when the same incident has been described with divergent numbers or data classes. A diverging narrative across regulators is a pattern that escalates enforcement severity.
value: Prevents the 'regulator cross-reference' problem that has escalated several prominent supervisory cases from technical fines to broader corporate sanction.
implementation: (1) Normalise submission events to carry canonical material-fact fields; (2) run 24h after any new submission for the incident_id; (3) alert DPO + legal if divergence found.
visualization: Table of divergent incidents, bar chart of divergence magnitude, single-value 'incidents with consistent submissions'.
knownFalsePositives: Genuine refinement of scope between the initial 72h notice and the 30-day follow-up is legitimate and expected; parametrise a tolerance window.
grandmaExplanation: We help you prevent the 'regulator cross-reference' problem that has escalated several prominent supervisory cases from technical fines to broader corporate sanction — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: regulator:submission sourcetype records with normalised material-fact fields (affected_subjects, data_classes, root_cause)..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalise submission events to carry canonical material-fact fields; (2) run 24h after any new submission for the incident_id; (3) alert DPO + legal if divergence found.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=notifications sourcetype=regulator:submission earliest=-30d
| stats values(affected_subjects) as subjects_set values(data_classes) as data_set dc(affected_subjects) as subjects_uniques dc(data_classes) as data_uniques by incident_id
| where subjects_uniques>1 OR data_uniques>1
| table incident_id subjects_set data_set
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions** — Prevents the 'regulator cross-reference' problem that has escalated several prominent supervisory cases from technical fines to broader corporate sanction.

Documented **Data sources**: regulator:submission sourcetype records with normalised material-fact fields (affected_subjects, data_classes, root_cause). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: notifications; **sourcetype**: regulator:submission. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=notifications, sourcetype=regulator:submission, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by incident_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where subjects_uniques>1 OR data_uniques>1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions**): table incident_id subjects_set data_set


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of divergent incidents, bar chart of divergence magnitude, single-value 'incidents with consistent submissions'.
